tools.name: pull
tools.description:
Build (or refresh) the local snip corpus from your Snipd account.

pull is the populate step every other command reads from: it fetches the
export catalog, downloads your snips through the sanctioned Obsidian export
API, and upserts them into a local SQLite mirror with a full-text index.
Run it once to seed the corpus, then again (optionally with --updated-after)
to pull in what changed. Read-only against Snipd; requires SNIPD_TOKEN.

tools.name: sql
tools.description: Run read-only SQL against local database. Use for ad-hoc analysis, aggregations, and joins across synced resources. Requires sync first.

params.name: query
params.description: SQL query (SELECT or WITH...SELECT). Synced records live in resources(resource_type, id, data); filter by resource_type and use json_extract on data, e.g. SELECT json_extract(data,'$.name') FROM resources WHERE resource_type='catalog'.

tools.name: sync
tools.description:
Sync data from the API into a local SQLite database. Supports resumable
incremental sync (only fetches new data since last sync) and full resync.
Once synced, use the 'search' command for instant full-text search.

Exit codes & warnings:
  Resources the API denies access to (HTTP 403, or HTTP 400 with an
  access-policy body) are reported as warnings rather than failing the
  run. In --json mode each is emitted as a {"event":"sync_warning",...}
  line carrying status, reason, and message fields, and a final
  {"event":"sync_summary",...} aggregates the run.

  Exit 0 when at least one resource synced and no resource flagged in
  the spec as critical (x-critical: true) failed; non-critical failures
  emit {"event":"sync_warning","reason":"exit_policy_default_changed",
  ...} so callers can detect that a partial failure was tolerated. Pass
  --strict to exit non-zero on any per-resource failure. Exit is always
  non-zero when every selected resource failed, regardless of --strict.

Resource scoping:
  --resources runs the named top-level resources, plus any parent-keyed
  dependent whose own name is listed OR whose parent table is listed.
  Naming a parent therefore cascades to its dependents (so "sync this
  parent and its children" works without listing every nested resource
  by hand). There is no flag today to suppress the cascade for a named
  parent. To run a dependent without re-syncing its parent, list only
  the dependent by name; the parent table must already be populated
  from a prior sync.

params.name: agent
params.description: Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes) (default: false)

params.name: compact
params.description: Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage (default: false)

params.name: concurrency
params.description: Number of parallel sync workers (default: 4)

params.name: csv
params.description: Output as CSV (table and array responses) (default: false)

params.name: data-source
params.description: Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default: auto)

params.name: db
params.description: SQLite database file path (default: resolved data directory data.db)

params.name: dry-run
params.description: Show request without sending (default: false)

params.name: full
params.description: Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint) (default: false)

params.name: global-param
params.description: Extra query param to inject into every sync request including dependent path-scoped calls (repeatable, key=value). Use when an API requires a scope on every call regardless of path nesting.

params.name: human-friendly
params.description: Enable colored output and rich formatting (default: false)

params.name: json
params.description: Output as JSON (default: false)

params.name: latest-only
params.description: Refresh head of each resource only; clears resume cursor and caps pages at 1. Mutually exclusive with --since (--since wins). (default: false)

params.name: max-age
params.description: Maximum acceptable age of local-store data before a stderr hint suggests sync; 0 disables (default: 30m0s)

params.name: max-pages
params.description: Maximum pages to fetch per resource (0 = unlimited; cap-hit emits a sync_warning event) (default: 0)

params.name: no-cache
params.description: Bypass response cache (default: false)

params.name: no-color
params.description: Disable colored output (default: false)

params.name: no-input
params.description: Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents) (default: false)

params.name: no-learn
params.description: Disable the teach/recall learning loop for this invocation (default: false)

params.name: no-prune
params.description: Disable deletion reconciliation on --full (by default a full sync prunes local rows the API no longer returns for a fully-enumerated parent partition) (default: false)

params.name: param
params.description: Extra query param to inject into flat-list sync requests (repeatable, key=value). Skipped on path-scoped dependent requests so a top-level scope like workspace=<id> does not double up on /parents/<id>/children calls. Use --global-param to inject everywhere. Avoid pagination keys (limit/since/cursor) — overriding them corrupts resume state.

params.name: plain
params.description: Output as plain tab-separated text (default: false)

params.name: quiet
params.description: Bare output, one value per line (default: false)

params.name: rate-limit
params.description: Max requests per second (0 to disable) (default: 0)

params.name: resource-param
params.description: Per-resource extra query param (repeatable, resource:key=value). Wins over --param and --global-param when keys conflict.

params.name: resources
params.description: Comma-separated resource types to sync. Naming a parent also runs its parent-keyed dependents (see Long help for scoping).

params.name: select
params.description: Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

params.name: since
params.description: Incremental sync duration (e.g. 7d, 24h, 1w, 30m)

params.name: strict
params.description: Exit non-zero on any per-resource failure (default: only critical failures or all-resource failure exit non-zero). (default: false)

params.name: timeout
params.description: Request timeout (default: 1m0s)

params.name: yes
params.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

tools.name: synthesize
tools.description:
Assemble a compact evidence feed for a concept across your snips — ranked by
full-text relevance, deduped, and projected to snippet fields (no transcript
unless --transcript). Use this to hand an agent exactly what it needs to
synthesize a cross-show answer without dragging whole transcripts through
context. Plain stemmed terms work best; the index is porter-stemmed.
